Functions for initializing, deinitializing, storing, and retrieving app names in a cache.

# Purpose
This C header file provides function declarations for managing an application name storage system, specifically for caching app names associated with app IDs. The function [`ancs_app_name_storage_init`](<#ancs_app_name_storage_init>) initializes the storage and allocates the necessary cache, while [`ancs_app_name_storage_deinit`](<#ancs_app_name_storage_deinit>) deinitializes the storage and frees the cache. The function [`ancs_app_name_storage_store`](<#ancs_app_name_storage_store>) allows storing an app name and app ID pair in the cache, with the capability to evict the oldest entry if the cache is full. The function [`ancs_app_name_storage_get`](<#ancs_app_name_storage_get>) retrieves an app name from the cache using the app ID as a key, returning a pointer to the cached data or `NULL` if the app name is not found. The file includes the `ancs_types.h` header, which likely defines the `ANCSAttribute` type used in the function parameters and return values.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `ancs_types.h`


# Function Declarations (Public API)

### ancs\_app\_name\_storage\_init<!-- {{#callable_declaration:ancs_app_name_storage_init}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../src/fw/comm/ble/kernel_le_client/ancs/ancs_app_name_storage.h#L22>)

Initialize app name storage and allocate cache.
- **Description**: Use this function to set up the storage system for app names. It allocates the necessary memory and initializes the cache for storing app name and app ID pairs. This function must be called before any other operations on the app name storage. Ensure that the storage is not already initialized before calling this function, as it asserts that the cache is not already allocated.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`ancs_app_name_storage_init`](<ancs_app_name_storage.c.md#ancs_app_name_storage_init>)  (Implementation)

### ancs\_app\_name\_storage\_deinit<!-- {{#callable_declaration:ancs_app_name_storage_deinit}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../src/fw/comm/ble/kernel_le_client/ancs/ancs_app_name_storage.h#L25>)

Deinitializes app name storage and frees associated cache.
- **Description**: Use this function to deinitialize the app name storage when it is no longer needed. This function releases any resources allocated during initialization, including the cache. It is important to call this function to prevent memory leaks. Ensure that no other operations on the app name storage are performed after calling this function, as the storage will be in an invalid state.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`ancs_app_name_storage_deinit`](<ancs_app_name_storage.c.md#ancs_app_name_storage_deinit>)  (Implementation)


---
### ancs\_app\_name\_storage\_store<!-- {{#callable_declaration:ancs_app_name_storage_store}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../src/fw/comm/ble/kernel_le_client/ancs/ancs_app_name_storage.h#L31>)

Store an app name and app ID pair in the cache.
- **Description**: Use this function to store a pair of app name and app ID attributes in the app name cache. This function must be called after the cache has been initialized with `ancs_app_name_storage_init`. If the cache is full, the function will evict the oldest entry to make space for the new pair. Ensure that both `app_id` and `app_name` are valid pointers before calling this function, as passing null pointers will result in no operation.
- **Inputs**:
    - `app_id`: The app ID attribute to use as a key. Must be a valid pointer and not null. The function does not perform any operation if this parameter is null.
    - `app_name`: The app name attribute to store. Must be a valid pointer and not null. The function does not perform any operation if this parameter is null.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`ancs_app_name_storage_store`](<ancs_app_name_storage.c.md#ancs_app_name_storage_store>)  (Implementation)


---
### ancs\_app\_name\_storage\_get<!-- {{#callable_declaration:ancs_app_name_storage_get}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../src/fw/comm/ble/kernel_le_client/ancs/ancs_app_name_storage.h#L39>)

Retrieve an app name from storage using an app ID.
- **Description**: Use this function to get the app name associated with a given app ID from the app name cache. It must be called after the cache has been initialized with `ancs_app_name_storage_init`. If the app ID is not found in the cache, the function returns `NULL`. The returned pointer is valid only as long as the cache remains unchanged; if the cache is invalidated, the pointer becomes invalid. To retain the app name, copy the data to a different location.
- **Inputs**:
    - `app_id`: The app ID attribute used as a key to search in the cache. Must not be null. The function returns `NULL` if the app ID is not found.
- **Output**: An `ANCSAttribute` pointer containing the app name if found, or `NULL` if not found.
- **See Also**: [`ancs_app_name_storage_get`](<ancs_app_name_storage.c.md#ancs_app_name_storage_get>)  (Implementation)
